hard.next_step: RFI Response Timelines: Questions Submitted ontime before 6/5/25 Answers in on 12 th June Users: Employees 3,800 Procurement Users 70 Casual / Self Service users 2,300 Spend Between 2.5 and 3.0 Billion CapEx 900 MM OpEx 1.6 to 2.5 Billion Annual Transactions: Purchase Requisitions 40,000 to 50,000 Purchase Orders 40,000 to 50,000 Invoices 168,000 Active Suppliers (Master Records) 1,285 Active Contracts 1,435 (Procurement) Active Contracts 900 (Commercial) Expected Contract Template counts - 30 Annual Sourcing Events per Year - 20 (and 10 are complex) Specific Function User Counts Supplier Management - 60 Individuals Contract Management - 85 Individuals (and the 2,300 casual users will not have access to CLM) Sourcing - 60 individuals Total Power Users including Legal or Business Users - 85 Individuals Target Date for QIT Submission is NLT 15th June Share with Implementation Team post QIT Response Target Date for Implementaiton sharing by NLT 22nd June Submission Deadline (Via Email): 6/22/2026 at 5:00 PM EDT Conference Room Demo’s begin 13 th July or so.Confirmed Intent to respond on 2nd June Questions are Due by June 5th Need Presales assignnment. Confirm intent to respond by June 5; presales review; submit vendor questions
hard.ais_why:
• PBF issued a formal Source-to-Pay RFP for a 'tech-enabled Procurement Operations transformation'; Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped, so AI capability is in play as part of the platform.
• The stated driver is broad S2P modernization across sourcing, contracting and operational spend rather than AI specifically; AI is not called out as the primary evaluation criterion.
• Largest deal in the set ($823K) at Formal Evaluation, but no agentic-platform discussion or AI-requirement rating is recorded, so appetite reads as open-but-unproven.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live, procedurally sound S2P RFP at PBF Energy: Zycus submitted its full proposal on 22 Jun and both buyer contacts (Diane Dunbar and Archland 'Joe' Bergeron) are highly responsive, with conference-room demos due to begin ~13 Jul and a scheduling decision expected by ~10 Jul. The relationship is still procurement-coordinator-only with no economic buyer mapped and no live buyer call ever recorded, so the win rests on breaking out of a paper-only evaluation at the demo.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 02 Jun 2026, 34 days in Formal Evaluation, 154 days to close (07 Dec 2026). Last SF activity 02 Jul (4 days ago), continuous email cadence 04 Jun to 02 Jul. RFP question-and-answer completed 12 Jun, QIT/implementation questionnaire returned, full proposal submitted on the 22 Jun 5pm EDT deadline. Forward pace requires demos to run mid-July, vendor selection Aug-Sep, commercials/negotiation Sep-Oct and contracting Nov to hold the December close; no forward slip in field history (Amount rose 250k to 823k on 24 Jun as scope was clarified up).
evidence: Full RFP proposal submitted on the 22 Jun 5pm EDT deadline; Diane Dunbar confirmed receipt same day (Task/email 22 Jun 2026)
Both buyer contacts highly responsive: Joe Bergeron replied 02 Jul that a conference-room-demo decision is expected 'towards the end of next week' (~10 Jul) (Task 02 Jul 2026)
Structured RFP process is intact: vendor questions in by 05 Jun, all answers returned 12 Jun, QIT and implementation timeline shared, demos to begin ~13 Jul (SF Next Step + Tasks 04-12 Jun 2026)
Amount clarified upward 250k to 823k on 24 Jun as full S2P scope firmed (Field History 24 Jun 2026)
Economic buyer unmapped and the competing shortlist unknown are real early-stage forecast risks, but neither is fatal at Formal Evaluation with a live, on-schedule process

metrics.narrative: PBF Energy runs procurement at large scale: $2.5-3.0B annual spend (CapEx ~$900M, OpEx $1.6-2.5B), 168,000 invoices/year, 40,000-50,000 purchase requisitions and POs, 3,800 employees, 70 procurement power users and 2,300 casual/self-service users across 1,285 active suppliers and ~2,335 active contracts (1,435 procurement, 900 commercial), with 20 sourcing events a year of which 10 are complex (SF Next Step, 22 Jun 2026). The stated business driver is a 'tech-enabled Procurement Operations transformation' across sourcing, contracting and operational spend (AIS_Why + RFP scope). What is NOT yet established is a quantified value case: no savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been stated by the buyer, so the $823K investment currently rests on scale and modernization intent rather than a hard ROI number. Quantifying that ROI with PBF's finance/procurement leadership is the open metrics move for the conference-room demo.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The RFP is run by procurement operations: Diane Dunbar (Sr. Procurement Coordinator, IT; Category Leader, Strategic Sourcing and Contract Management per her email signature) is the primary point of contact and Archland 'Joe' Bergeron (Sr. Procurement Coordinator) manages the question-and-answer logistics (Tasks/emails 04 Jun-02 Jul 2026). No Salesforce OpportunityContactRole, MEDDPICC field, or activity names a budget owner or an executive sponsor, and no VP Procurement, CFO or CPO has appeared in any thread. At an $823K S2P transformation the true economic buyer sits above these coordinators (likely a VP Procurement/CFO), so identifying and reaching that person before commercials is a priority; on current evidence budget authority is genuinely unknown, not merely unlogged.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are visible from the RFP mechanics even without a buyer call. PBF is running a formal written RFP with a vendor question round, a QIT (Questionnaire for Implementation Team) requiring an implementation timeline, mandatory conference-room demos driven by buyer-supplied use cases sent two weeks in advance, and a requirement for three energy or oil-and-gas sector customer references (Tasks 04-22 Jun 2026). Two concrete technical criteria surfaced on 11 Jun: integration to SAP must go via Boomi rather than direct APIs, and the CLM must support possible ITAR / EAR clause handling ('need to use Boomi rather than direct APIs to SAP; also possible ITAR / EAR clause support in CLM', Task 11 Jun 2026). Fit against PBF's business-requirement use cases and O&G reference credibility will therefore weigh heavily; the full weighted scoring model is not yet visible.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a disciplined, date-driven RFP process and Zycus has kept pace at every gate: intent to respond confirmed 02 Jun, vendor questions submitted by 05 Jun, all RFP answers returned by 12 Jun, QIT and implementation timeline shared, and the full proposal submitted on the 22 Jun 5pm EDT deadline (Tasks/emails 02-22 Jun 2026). The next stage is conference-room demos beginning ~13 Jul, built around use cases PBF will circulate two weeks ahead, with PBF indicating on 02 Jul that the demo schedule will be decided 'towards the end of next week' (~10 Jul) (Task 02 Jul 2026). Diane Dunbar has asked that future communications route through the proper RFP channel, signalling a controlled, procurement-governed process. The approval chain above procurement (who signs off vendor selection) is not yet documented.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view: as a New Business S2P deal a full MSA plus Order Form and a separately-signed SOW will be required, but no legal, InfoSec, or procurement-onboarding conversation has started this early in the RFP. Two forward-looking paper signals already exist: PBF wants SAP integration delivered through Boomi (a middleware/scoping consideration for the SOW) and possible ITAR / EAR export-control clause support in the CLM, which will draw legal and compliance review given PBF's oil-and-gas/regulated profile (Task 11 Jun 2026). Visibility here will come once vendor selection narrows and PBF exposes its procurement/legal owners; there is no contract-expiration forcing function on record.

identify_pain.narrative: The articulated pain is a fragmented, largely manual Source-to-Pay operation that PBF wants to modernize into a tech-enabled procurement operating model spanning sourcing, contracting and operational spend (AIS_Why + RFP scope). The scale figures make the pain concrete: 168,000 invoices, 40,000-50,000 POs and requisitions and ~2,335 active contracts a year are being managed without a unified S2P platform (SF Next Step, 22 Jun 2026), and the explicit need to bridge to SAP via Boomi plus support ITAR/EAR clauses in CLM points to real integration and compliance friction today (Task 11 Jun 2026). The pain is documented at process level but has not been voiced by a named business owner on a call (no Avoma call exists for this deal), so the individual pain owner and the emotional urgency behind the RFP remain to be confirmed at the demo.

champion.narrative: There is a responsive coordinating contact but not yet an internal advocate. Diane Dunbar (Sr. Procurement Coordinator, IT) is the primary contact and RFP lead and Archland 'Joe' Bergeron (Sr. Procurement Coordinator) is the day-to-day counterpart; the owner noted on 02 Jul that 'both he and Diane have been highly responsive' (Task 02 Jul 2026). That is strong procedural engagement, but neither has demonstrated advocacy: no introduction to a budget owner, no competitive intelligence shared, no internal business-case help, and no urgency beyond the RFP calendar. Their role is coordinator/evaluator, not decision-maker, and their access to the economic buyer is unproven. Kelsey Beans Mckell (Procurement Manager) is the most senior named contact but has been passive with no activity on record. Converting Diane or Joe into a genuine champion, or surfacing a sourcing/CLM power user who will carry Zycus internally, is the key relationship move at the ~13 Jul demo.

competition.narrative: SAP is the standing competitive reference point: PBF runs SAP ECC (confirmed by the 11 Jun requirement to integrate via Boomi rather than direct APIs to SAP) and SAP was named as a considered S2P option in prior living memory ('SAP is obviously one we're looking at given our ERP'), giving SAP a native-integration and incumbency angle. Beyond SAP, this is a formal multi-vendor RFP, so a competitive shortlist almost certainly exists, but no other rival has been named in any Salesforce field, email, task, or call this run, and no buyer call has ever been recorded to surface it, so the rest of the field is genuinely unknown rather than empty. Zycus wins by out-depthing SAP on best-of-breed sourcing and CLM plus Merlin/Agentic AI, and by de-risking the SAP estate with a clean Boomi integration story; uncovering who else is on the shortlist is a priority.

deal_movement.summary: Live and worked within the last week. Zycus completed the full RFP question-and-answer cycle and submitted its proposal on the 22 Jun deadline; the buyer confirmed receipt and, as of 02 Jul, is deciding the conference-room demo schedule (expected ~10 Jul). Deal size was clarified upward to $823K on 24 Jun. The next real gate is the mid-July demo.

items.change: Amount clarified upward from $250K to $823K as full S2P scope firmed
Full RFP proposal submitted to PBF on the 22 Jun 5pm EDT deadline; Diane Dunbar confirmed receipt
All RFP question batches answered and returned to PBF (QIT/implementation questionnaire completed)
Buyer surfaced technical requirements: Boomi-based SAP integration and possible ITAR/EAR clause support in CLM
Joe Bergeron indicated conference-room-demo decision expected ~10 Jul; both PBF contacts highly responsive

competitive_position.summary: SAP is the strongest identifiable current threat: PBF runs SAP ECC (proven by the 11 Jun Boomi-to-SAP integration requirement) and SAP carries native-integration and incumbency leverage into an S2P decision. This is a formal multi-vendor RFP so other vendors are likely on the shortlist, but none has been named in any field, email, task, or call, so the rest of the field is unknown, not absent. Zycus's wedge is best-of-breed sourcing and CLM depth plus Merlin/Agentic AI, with a clean Boomi integration to de-risk the SAP estate.

competitors.name: SAP
competitors.quote: need to use Boomi rather than direct APIs to SAP
competitors.source: Task 11 Jun 2026 (SAP ECC estate confirmed) + prior sweep living memory (SAP named as an S2P option)
competitors.how_we_win: Out-depth SAP on best-of-breed sourcing and CLM plus Merlin/Agentic AI, and neutralize the integration objection with a proven Boomi-to-SAP connector so PBF keeps SAP as system-of-record while gaining a stronger S2P layer.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: PBF's stated expectations are broad S2P transformation, clean integration to its SAP ECC estate, and regulated-industry compliance in CLM. Zycus is aligned on suite breadth and integration but must prove ITAR/EAR CLM handling and O&G reference credibility at the demo.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy priorities for a paper-only RFP that is still procurement-coordinator-only.
flags: Turn the ~13 Jul conference-room demo into a differentiated O&G S2P story: lead with Boomi-to-SAP integration and ITAR/EAR CLM to break out of a paper-only evaluation.
Map and reach the economic buyer / budget owner above Diane Dunbar and Archland Bergeron before commercials open.
Multi-thread beyond procurement coordinators to a sourcing/CLM power user and a senior procurement sponsor to build a real champion.

items.name: Diane Dunbar
items.title: Sr. Procurement Coordinator, IT
items.risk: Developing, not confirmed. If she stays a neutral RFP coordinator the deal turns on product and price alone, giving SAP its integration/incumbency opening.
items.sentiment: Procedurally engaged and responsive; primary RFP contact and Category Leader for Strategic Sourcing and Contract Management per her email signature, but no advocacy demonstrated yet.

items.name: Archland Bergeron
items.title: Sr. Procurement Coordinator
items.risk: Helpful on logistics but no evidence of internal influence or budget access; not yet a decision-level relationship.
items.sentiment: Highly responsive day-to-day counterpart (signs emails 'Joe Bergeron'); manages RFP question logistics and shares process updates.

items.name: Kelsey Beans Mckell
items.title: Procurement Manager
items.risk: Potential decision-maker or sponsor left untouched; single-threading through coordinators leaves the senior procurement relationship unbuilt.
items.sentiment: Most senior named contact but passive, with no activity on record this cycle.

champion_strength.summary: Diane Dunbar is a responsive, procedurally-engaged RFP lead but not yet an advocate: she coordinates the process and replies promptly (with Joe Bergeron), yet has not introduced a budget owner, shared competitive intelligence, or pushed Zycus internally. She is a developing champion at best; the ~13 Jul conference-room demo is the moment to convert her (or a sourcing/CLM power user) by proving Zycus solves PBF's S2P pain better than SAP and offering to co-build the internal business case.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped in the S2P suite, but AI is not the buyer's stated primary driver and no agentic-platform discussion is on record. AIS reads Moderate (5.0); the AI story is in-scope but under-activated with the buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5.0 / Moderate: PBF's driver is broad S2P modernization rather than AI specifically, though Merlin, Merlin Intake and Agentic AI are scoped. No call evidence exists to confirm or raise AI appetite; treat as open-but-unproven and probe at the demo.

Single-threaded through procurement coordinators; the senior Procurement Manager (Kelsey Beans Mckell) and any executive sponsor are untouched.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement pulse is LIVE: continuous email cadence 04 Jun-02 Jul, proposal delivered on deadline, both buyer contacts responsive, next gate (demo scheduling) imminent. Confidence in the win is tempered only by the unmapped economic buyer and unknown shortlist, not by cadence.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Live, on-schedule S2P RFP at PBF Energy: Zycus is in with a full 22 Jun proposal and two highly responsive procurement contacts, but the relationship is coordinator-only with no economic buyer mapped and no live buyer call yet, so the ~13 Jul conference-room demo is the moment that decides whether this becomes a real shortlist position.

win_position.text: Zycus is a credible, fully-responsive bidder: it met every RFP gate and submitted its proposal on the 22 Jun deadline, and Joe Bergeron confirmed on 02 Jul that both he and Diane Dunbar have been highly responsive.
No economic buyer or budget owner is mapped; the whole relationship sits with two procurement coordinators, so Zycus has no line yet to the person who signs off vendor selection.
SAP holds the incumbency and native-integration angle because PBF runs SAP ECC (proven by the 11 Jun Boomi-to-SAP requirement), and the rest of the competing shortlist is still unknown.

deal_momentum.text: Continuous cadence from 04 Jun to 02 Jul with a proposal delivered on deadline and a demo decision expected ~10 Jul keeps the process moving on the buyer's own timeline.
Momentum is still rep-driven and paper-based: no live buyer call has ever been held, so fit and champion strength remain unvalidated ahead of the demo.

customer_commitment.text: PBF committed real process to Zycus, running a formal QIT, requesting O&G references, and scheduling conference-room demos with use cases sent two weeks in advance.
Commitment is procedural, not preferential: Diane asked that communications route through the RFP channel and no contact has signalled a lean toward Zycus, so this reads as arms-length evaluation.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through procurement coordinators with the senior Procurement Manager (Kelsey Beans Mckell) passive and no executive sponsor engaged.
Unproven Boomi-to-SAP integration and ITAR/EAR CLM support are the technical risks SAP can exploit if the demo does not land them.

champion_strength.evidence: Diane Dunbar is responsive and coordinating but shows no advocacy, budget access, or competitive intel; developing, not a confirmed champion.

economic_buyer.evidence: No budget owner named anywhere; relationship is coordinator-only.

customer_preference.evidence: Arms-length RFP process; buyer routed communications through a formal channel and voiced no lean toward Zycus.
Arms-length formal RFP: 'We will be in touch if any clarification is needed... For all future communications, please ' (Diane Dunbar, 10 Jun 2026). No stated preference for Zycus.

business_case.evidence: Large operational scale documented ($2.5-3.0B spend, 168K invoices, 40-50K POs/PRs, ~2,335 contracts) and a transformation mandate, but no ROI, savings, or cycle-time figure quantified by the buyer.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by momentum 59 vs stage-expected 52; champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 21 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: PBF Energy — Formal Evaluation · $823K · closes 7 Dec
cro_panel.intro: Live, on-schedule S2P RFP at PBF Energy; Zycus is in with a full 22 Jun proposal and two highly responsive procurement contacts, but the relationship is coordinator-only with no economic buyer mapped and no live buyer call yet, so the ~13 Jul conference-room demo is the moment that decides whether this becomes a real shortlist position

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Zycus is a credible, fully-responsive bidder; it met every RFP gate and submitted its proposal on the 22 Jun deadline, and Joe Bergeron confirmed on 02 Jul that both he and Diane Dunbar have been highly responsive
No economic buyer or budget owner is mapped; the whole relationship sits with two procurement coordinators, so Zycus has no line yet to the person who signs off vendor selection
SAP holds the incumbency and native-integration angle because PBF runs SAP ECC (proven by the 11 Jun Boomi-to-SAP requirement), and the rest of the competing shortlist is still unknown

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Continuous cadence from 04 Jun to 02 Jul with a proposal delivered on deadline and a demo decision expected ~10 Jul keeps the process moving on the buyer's own timeline
Momentum is still rep-driven and paper-based; no live buyer call has ever been held, so fit and champion strength remain unvalidated ahead of the demo

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Single-threaded through procurement coordinators with the senior Procurement Manager (Kelsey Beans Mckell) passive and no executive sponsor engaged
Unproven Boomi-to-SAP integration and ITAR/EAR CLM support are the technical risks SAP can exploit if the demo does not land them

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SAP is the strongest identifiable current threat: PBF runs SAP ECC (proven by the 11 Jun Boomi-to-SAP integration requirement) and SAP carries native-integration and incumbency leverage into an S2P decision.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: PBF committed real process to Zycus, running a formal QIT, requesting O&G references, and scheduling conference-room demos with use cases sent two weeks in advance
Commitment is procedural, not preferential; Diane asked that communications route through the RFP channel and no contact has signalled a lean toward Zycus, so this reads as arms-length evaluation
